title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Joseph Aoun, president of Northeastern University, earned over $3 million in total compensation for Fiscal Year 2025.
Aoun's compensation increase included $700,000 in deferred compensation and $550,000 in bonus and incentives.
Aoun's earnings surpassed MIT President Sally Kornbluth by nearly $1 million in FY25.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound at Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge.
Police received a 911 call at 5:30 a.m.; Bautista was shot shortly before 4:30 a.m.
Cambridge police and Massachusetts State Police are investigating and seek public help via anonymous tips.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major from New England Aquarium joined efforts in South Africa to help endangered penguin chicks.
Major volunteered at SANCCOB's penguin nursery, caring for about 100 chicks needing intensive care.
African penguins face extinction by 2035 due to food scarcity, habitat loss, and competition with fisheries.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras appeals a seven-game suspension after a benches-clearing incident with the Nationals.
Contreras emotionally apologized, reflecting on his actions during recent games amid personal struggles from disasters in Venezuela.
Contreras seeks his fourth All-Star recognition, having hit a key home run against the Angels despite a challenging week.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected for the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Chapman achieved a 2.10 ERA with 33 strikeouts and 17 saves over 25.2 innings this season.
Suarez has a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts in 88.2 innings, heading to the All-Star Game in Philadelphia.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce expresses understanding and disappointment regarding Celtics' trade of Jaylen Brown to the 76ers.
Pierce encourages Brown to use feelings of disrespect as motivation for his future success in the NBA.
Brown criticizes Celtics, particularly Brad Stevens, for lack of respect and poor communication during trade talks.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston marked Independence Day with a parade and Declaration reading, celebrating the 250th anniversary of the document's signing.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular featured performances by Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper at the Hatch Memorial Shell.
The fireworks event included a drone show and was attended by Massachusetts Gov. Maura Healey and Jane Lynch.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin has the best OPS (.977) on the Red Sox in the past 30 days, leading the team offensively.
Durbin hit his seventh home run in the last month and ranks second in RBIs and hits for the Red Sox.
Boston has team control over Durbin until 2031, offering roster flexibility with his current performance surge.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman set the MLB record for most strikeouts by a relief pitcher, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm with his 1,364th strikeout.
Chapman achieved the milestone during a 5-2 victory over the Los Angeles Angels, striking out Denzer Guzman in the ninth inning.
Having struck out 118 batters since joining in 2025, Chapman's record highlights his dominance, needing just 847 innings to Wilhelm's 1,872.1.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranked the most collision-prone city in the U.S., averaging 3.7 years between collisions.
Two Massachusetts cities, Worcester and Springfield, placed 197th and 196th with drivers averaging 5.14 and 5.18 years between crashes.
Brownsville, Texas, named the safest U.S. city for drivers, averaging nearly 15 years between collisions.
